title: Ultra-Creamy Potato Puree
intro: This silky, buttery potato puree is so smooth it practically pours from the spoon. A generous ribbon of cold butter whipped into warm potatoes makes it feel almost like velvet.
ingredients: 2 lb (900 g) small yellow potatoes, unpeeled
1 tablespoon fine sea salt, plus more to taste
10 oz (280 g) cold unsalted butter, cut into cubes
steps: Place the whole unpeeled potatoes in a large pot, cover with cold water, add the salt, and bring to a gentle simmer.
Cook until a knife slides in with no resistance, about 25 to 30 minutes, then drain and let them steam-dry for a minute.
Peel the warm potatoes carefully and pass them through a food mill or ricer back into the dry pot.
Set the pot over low heat and stir the potatoes with a wooden spoon for a minute to drive off extra moisture.
Beat in the cold butter a few cubes at a time, waiting until each addition melts before adding more.
Pour in the warm milk gradually, stirring vigorously until the puree is glossy and smooth.
Season with nutmeg, white pepper, and additional salt, then serve immediately while hot.
tip: For an even finer texture, push the finished puree through a fine-mesh sieve one last time before serving.

title: Roasted Carrots with Herbed Yogurt
intro: Caramelized whole carrots meet a cool, garlicky yogurt swirl and a scattering of toasted seeds. It is bright, earthy, and endlessly satisfying next to almost anything.
ingredients: 2 lb (900 g) medium carrots, scrubbed and halved lengthwise
Salt and black pepper to taste
2 tablespoons chopped fresh dill and mint
steps: Heat the oven to 425 F (220 C) and line a large baking sheet with parchment.
Toss the carrots with olive oil, cumin, honey, salt, and pepper until evenly coated.
Spread them in a single layer and roast until deeply golden and tender, about 30 to 35 minutes, turning once.
Stir together the yogurt, grated garlic, half the herbs, and a pinch of salt in a small bowl.
Spread the herbed yogurt across a serving platter using the back of a spoon.
Pile the warm carrots over the yogurt, letting some juices mingle.
Finish with the remaining herbs and the toasted pumpkin seeds before serving.
tip: Choose carrots of similar thickness so they roast evenly and finish at the same time.

title: Parmesan and Herb Roasted Potatoes
intro: Crisp-edged potatoes tumbled with garlic, fresh rosemary, and a shower of Parmesan. These are the kind of golden, savory bites that vanish from the pan before dinner is served.
ingredients: 2.5 lb (1.1 kg) baby Yukon Gold potatoes, halved
steps: Heat the oven to 425 F (220 C) and place a large rimmed baking sheet inside to warm.
Toss the halved potatoes with olive oil, garlic, rosemary, salt, and pepper in a large bowl.
Spread the potatoes cut-side down on the hot baking sheet in a single layer.
Roast undisturbed for 25 minutes so the cut faces develop a deep golden crust.
Turn the potatoes, sprinkle over the Parmesan, and roast for another 15 to 20 minutes until crisp.
Transfer to a serving bowl and toss with fresh parsley.
Taste, add a final pinch of salt if needed, and serve warm.
tip: Preheating the pan gives the potatoes an instant sear and prevents them from sticking.

title: Buttery Braised Leeks
intro: Tender leeks slowly braised in butter and stock until meltingly soft and lightly glazed. This humble French side turns an underused vegetable into something quietly elegant.
ingredients: 6 medium leeks, white and pale green parts only
3/4 cup (180 ml) chicken or vegetable stock
Salt and black pepper to taste
steps: Trim the leeks, halve them lengthwise, and rinse thoroughly between the layers to remove grit.
Melt the butter with the olive oil in a wide skillet over medium heat.
Lay the leeks cut-side down and cook until lightly browned, about 4 minutes.
Pour in the stock, add the thyme, and season with salt and pepper.
Cover and braise gently over low heat until the leeks are completely tender, about 20 minutes.
Uncover and let the liquid reduce to a light glaze, spooning it over the leeks.
Brighten with a squeeze of lemon juice just before serving.
tip: Keep the root end intact while braising so each leek half holds together in the pan.

title: Charred Broccolini with Chili and Garlic
intro: Blistered broccolini tossed with sizzling garlic, a whisper of chili, and a bright hit of lemon. Fast, bold, and full of smoky char from a screaming-hot pan.
ingredients: Zest and juice of half a lemon
steps: Bring a pot of salted water to a boil and blanch the broccolini for 90 seconds.
Drain well and pat the stems dry so they will char rather than steam.
Heat the olive oil in a large skillet over medium-high until shimmering.
Add the broccolini and cook without moving until the edges blister, about 3 minutes.
Toss in the garlic and chili flakes and stir until fragrant and golden, about 1 minute.
Season with salt, then add the lemon zest and juice off the heat.
Scatter the Pecorino over the top and serve right away.
tip: Make sure the broccolini is dry before it hits the oil so it blisters instead of stewing.

title: Classic Potato Gratin Dauphinois
intro: Thin layers of potato bathed in garlicky cream and baked until bubbling and bronzed. This rich French gratin is comfort food at its most refined.
steps: Heat the oven to 375 F (190 C) and rub a baking dish all over with the cut garlic and butter.
Slice the potatoes into thin, even rounds using a mandoline or sharp knife.
Warm the cream and milk in a large pot with the salt, nutmeg, and white pepper.
Add the potato slices and simmer gently for 8 minutes so they begin to soften.
Layer half the potatoes in the dish, scatter with half the Gruyere, then repeat with the rest.
Pour the warm cream over the top and press the potatoes down so they are just submerged.
Bake until the top is deeply golden and the center is tender, about 55 to 60 minutes.
Let the gratin rest for 15 minutes so it sets before serving.
tip: Simmering the potatoes in the cream first ensures they cook evenly and never turn out chalky.

title: Brown Butter Mashed Sweet Potatoes
intro: Fluffy sweet potatoes whipped smooth with nutty brown butter and a touch of maple. Cozy, subtly sweet, and a guaranteed crowd-pleaser at any holiday table.
ingredients: 3 lb (1.4 kg) sweet potatoes, peeled and cubed
steps: Place the sweet potato cubes in a large pot, cover with cold salted water, and bring to a boil.
Cook until completely fork-tender, about 15 to 18 minutes, then drain thoroughly.
Melt the butter in a small saucepan over medium heat, swirling until it turns golden and smells nutty.
Return the drained potatoes to the warm pot and mash until mostly smooth.
Stir in the brown butter, warm milk, maple syrup, cinnamon, salt, and cayenne.
Whip with a spoon or hand mixer until creamy and fluffy.
Taste and adjust the salt, then serve warm.
tip: Watch the brown butter closely near the end, as it goes from nutty to burnt in seconds.

title: Roasted Brussels Sprouts with Balsamic Glaze
intro: Crispy, caramelized Brussels sprouts finished with a sweet-tart balsamic reduction. The crackly edges and glossy glaze make even skeptics reach for seconds.
ingredients: 2 lb (900 g) Brussels sprouts, trimmed and halved
steps: Heat the oven to 425 F (220 C) and line a baking sheet with parchment.
Toss the Brussels sprouts with olive oil, salt, and pepper until well coated.
Arrange them cut-side down in a single layer for maximum browning.
Roast until crisp and deeply caramelized, about 25 to 30 minutes.
Meanwhile, simmer the balsamic vinegar and honey in a small pot until reduced and syrupy, about 6 minutes.
Transfer the roasted sprouts to a bowl and drizzle with the balsamic glaze.
Toss gently, top with Parmesan, and serve warm.
tip: Do not crowd the pan, or the sprouts will steam and never reach that crispy caramelized edge.

title: Truffled Macaroni and Cheese Gratin
intro: An elevated take on macaroni and cheese, with a silky triple-cheese sauce and a crisp buttered crumb crust. A hint of truffle turns a nursery classic into something dinner-party worthy.
steps: Heat the oven to 400 F (200 C) and cook the macaroni one minute short of al dente, then drain.
Melt 3 tablespoons butter in a large saucepan and whisk in the flour to form a smooth roux.
Gradually pour in the warm milk, whisking constantly until the sauce thickens, about 5 minutes.
Off the heat, stir in the cheddar, Gruyere, half the Parmesan, Dijon, and truffle oil until smooth.
Fold in the drained macaroni and season generously with salt and pepper, then spread into a buttered baking dish.
Melt the remaining butter and toss with the panko and remaining Parmesan.
Scatter the buttered crumbs over the top and bake until bubbling and golden, about 20 minutes.
Rest for 10 minutes before serving so the sauce sets slightly.
tip: Warm the milk before adding it to the roux to keep the cheese sauce lump-free and glossy.

title: Perfect Duck-Fat Roast Potatoes
intro: Shatteringly crisp on the outside and fluffy within, these roast potatoes are the gold standard of the Sunday roast. Parboiling and roughing up the edges is the secret to that legendary crunch.
ingredients: 3 lb (1.4 kg) Maris Piper or russet potatoes, peeled
steps: Heat the oven to 425 F (220 C) and cut the potatoes into large, even chunks.
Boil them in salted water for 8 minutes until the outsides are just soft, then drain in a colander.
Shake the colander firmly to rough up the edges into a fluffy, floury coating.
Heat the duck fat in a roasting tray in the oven until shimmering hot.
Carefully add the potatoes, turning to coat, and roast for 30 minutes without disturbing.
Add the garlic and thyme, turn the potatoes, and roast another 25 minutes until deeply golden.
Drain on paper towels, finish with flaky salt, and serve immediately.
tip: Getting the fat smoking-hot before the potatoes go in is what creates that instant crisp shell.

title: Green Beans with Toasted Almonds and Shallots
intro: Crisp-tender green beans tossed with buttery toasted almonds and sweet frizzled shallots. A timeless French almondine that dresses up any plate with minimal fuss.
ingredients: Salt and black pepper to taste
steps: Bring a large pot of salted water to a boil and prepare a bowl of ice water.
Blanch the green beans until bright and crisp-tender, about 4 minutes, then plunge into the ice bath.
Melt the butter in a large skillet over medium heat and add the sliced almonds.
Stir until the almonds are golden and fragrant, then add the shallots and cook until softened.
Drain the green beans well and add them to the skillet.
Toss until warmed through and coated in the nutty butter, about 3 minutes.
Finish with lemon juice, salt, and pepper, then serve.
tip: Shocking the beans in ice water locks in their vivid green color and snappy texture.

title: Herbed Couscous with Pomegranate and Pistachios
intro: Fluffy couscous studded with jewel-like pomegranate seeds, fresh herbs, and crunchy pistachios. It is fragrant, colorful, and every bit as good at room temperature.
ingredients: 1 cup chopped fresh parsley and mint
Salt and black pepper to taste
steps: Place the couscous in a large bowl and stir in one tablespoon of the olive oil.
Pour the hot stock over the couscous, cover tightly, and let it stand for 8 minutes.
Fluff the grains thoroughly with a fork to separate them and release steam.
Whisk the remaining olive oil with the lemon zest and juice, then pour over the couscous.
Fold in the chopped herbs, pomegranate seeds, and most of the pistachios.
Season with salt and pepper and toss until evenly combined.
Scatter the reserved pistachios over the top and serve warm or at room temperature.
tip: Fluffing the couscous with a fork rather than a spoon keeps the grains light and prevents clumping.
